stem: Tank A initially contains 800 liters of water and loses water at a rate of 12 liters per hour. Tank B initially contains 200 liters of water and gains water at a rate of 8 liters per hour. After how many hours will both tanks contain the same amount of water?
explanation: Let t be the time in hours. Tank A has 800 - 12t liters, and Tank B has 200 + 8t liters. Setting them equal: 800 - 12t = 200 + 8t. Adding 12t to both sides: 800 = 200 + 20t. Subtracting 200: 600 = 20t. Dividing by 20: t = 30 hours. Verification: Tank A has 800 - 12(30) = 800 - 360 = 440 liters. Tank B has 200 + 8(30) = 200 + 240 = 440 liters.

distractorRationale.A: Found when Tank A equals Tank B's initial amount. 800 - 12t = 200 gives 12t = 600, so t = 50, but made error = 20.
distractorRationale.B: Used the difference in rates incorrectly. Found (800 - 200) ÷ (12 + 8) = 600 ÷ 24 = 25.
distractorRationale.C: Correct answer: set 800 - 12t = 200 + 8t, solved 600 = 20t to get t = 30 hours.
distractorRationale.D: Used only Tank A's rate to find when it loses 600 liters. 600 ÷ 12 = 50, made arithmetic error = 40.
